description: Line chart on a white background titled 'Legal entries by asylum seekers at US-Mexico legal entry points are down 99.9%' with a subtitle 'At southwest ports by type, Oct. 2016-Mar. 2026'. Two plotted lines, an orange line labeled 'Asylum Seekers' and a blue line labeled 'Total Inadmissibles', run left to right with the area beneath shaded gray. The y-axis runs from 0 to about 50K and the x-axis is labeled by year from Jan '17 to Jan '26. Vertical reference lines divide the timeline into segments labeled Obama, Trump, Biden, and Trump. The lines stay low through 2017-2021, rise to a peak above 45K-50K around 2023-2024, then drop sharply to near zero after the final 'Trump' marker around Jan '25. Source notes cite CBP data and the Cato Institute logo appears at lower right.
notable_text: Legal entries by asylum seekers at US-Mexico legal entry points are down 99.9%; At southwest ports by type, Oct. 2016-Mar. 2026; Asylum Seekers; Total Inadmissibles; Obama Trump Biden Trump; CATO INSTITUTE
